opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My leg is burning and red and it's been getting worse all week, but I couldn't bring myself to go to the hospital — they never listen to me and I always get treated like I'm making it up. I feel hot and my heart's been racing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The red area on my leg keeps spreading and I feel feverish, but I kept putting off getting help because clinics make me panic and I expect to be dismissed.

symptom_narrative.onset: a minor scrape on my lower leg about a week ago that has slowly turned red, hot and painful and keeps spreading outward.
symptom_narrative.duration: about one week, and it has been getting worse.
symptom_narrative.character: burning, hot, tight and very tender; the red patch keeps enlarging.
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it has been — really painful, and I feel unwell and feverish.
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my right lower leg, below the knee.
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, steadily spreading over the week.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing; I have been putting a herbal poultice on it but it has not helped.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it keeps spreading on its own, and even light touch is very painful.
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling hot and sweaty, heart racing, and very anxious about getting seen.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: it is hard to walk on the leg, and the fear of going in for care is overwhelming.

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing intense anxiety and panic whenever I am in a clinic or hospital
past_medical_history.how_managed: I avoid medical settings as much as I can

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: past experiences of feeling dismissed and not believed by clinicians

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a home-made herbal poultice I have been putting on the leg

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Deeply distrusts the healthcare system after past experiences of bias and being dismissed; deliberately delays or avoids care; brings a binder of her own medical records to appointments so she will be believed; clinical environments trigger panic. She refused to attend an emergency department for this.

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: Wants above all to be believed and taken seriously; will disengage or refuse in-person care if she feels invalidated, minimised, or coerced.

communication_style.typical_message_style: Guarded and defensive, over-prepared with her own documentation, pre-emptively anticipating that she will be dismissed.

reluctance_topics: attending a hospital or emergency department

access_barriers.other: Severe institutional mistrust and clinical-environment panic driving deliberate care avoidance.

objective_data_offered.value: a large area of bright red, shiny, swollen skin on the right lower leg that looks hot and tense
38.2 degrees measured on my home thermometer
the skin over the red area feels hot to touch and is very tender
my heart feels like it is racing and pounding
a herbal poultice and a thick binder of my past medical records are on the table next to me
